name: Mesh Loader
description: Load mesh data from OBJ files into GPU textures.

mesh0._role: STATIC mesh surface triplet (reference 04 §8). positions: xyz worldpos, w=valid flag. normals: xyz, w unused. uvs: uv, zw unused. Uploaded CPU-side by the runtime (uploadMeshData) from the OBJ named by externalMesh / builtinMeshes; NOT ping-ponged, NOT recreated on resize. meshLoader's preview pass SAMPLES global_mesh0_positions and global_mesh0_normals; downstream meshRender rasterizes the triplet via DrawProcedural over SV_VertexID.

_notes.comment: Single fullscreen 'preview' pass that visualizes the mesh0 surface triplet (left half = positions remapped -1..1->0..1; right half = normals). No geometry/scatter/raymarch/volume-write pass: the actual mesh data upload is CPU-side (runtime uploadMeshData), driven by the demo UI loading an OBJ URL into the mesh0 surfaces. No scalar uniforms (definition.js globals = {}); only engine globals (resolution/tileOffset/fullResolution) are used, sampled UV = (fragCoord + tileOffset) / fullResolution per the WGSL. No MRT (single fragColor). No repeat. drawMode = fullscreen-triangle (default).
_notes.shaderGraph: SKIPPED — mesh/3D tier, depends on a CPU-side mesh upload and the mesh0 surface-triplet binding; ships as a runtime-rendered Texture2D, no Shader Graph Custom Function wrapper.
